summary: Clean soundboard recording captures a strong second set highlighted by soaring Eyes of the World, an extended Other One jam, and standout Bruce Hornsby performances.
review: The SBD source is generally crisp and full‑bodied, with just enough ambient crowd to retain the live feel. Musically the show shines in the second set – sharp transitions, a superb Eyes of the World jam, a marathon Space→Other One excursion and several fire‑brand Hornsby moments – while the first set is viewed as comparatively flat. Overall the recording is listenable and the performance is well worth revisiting.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 4‑5 stars, praising both the sound quality and many standout songs; a few criticisms about first‑set energy and Hornsby’s accordion do not outweigh the overall enthusiasm.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clean, full SBD mix with pleasant crowd ambience; some reviewers note a cassette‑like hiss and occasional muddiness when Hornsby’s squeezebox dominates the mix.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is compelling with sharp transitions (UJB → Saint) and extended jams; first set is considered ho‑hum by several listeners.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for crisp guitar tone, lyrical delivery and inventive midi jams, especially during the Other One and space jam.
band_member_comments.Phil: His deep, crisp bass shines on the extended Other One jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Mixed remarks – piano work is praised, but many feel Hornsby’s accordion/squeezebox is over‑prominent and muddies parts of the first set.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill and Mickey earn kudos for a dynamic drum solo after Eyes and an early return during the Other One jam.
